name: Chocoholics Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Toast the pecans and walnuts for 10-12 minutes or until they develop a toasty aroma. Remove from oven and set aside to cool.
2. Increase oven temperature to 350°F (175°C). Place unsweetened chocolate, 12 oz semi-sweet chocolate, butter, margarine, and espresso in the top of a double boiler over simmering (not boiling) water until chocolate is about 3/4 melted.
3. In a large mixing bowl, beat eggs with a wire whisk. Gradually add sugar, whisking until mixture becomes thick and light in color.
4. Remove chocolate mixture from heat and stir until completely melted and satin in appearance. Gradually whisk chocolate into egg mixture.
5. Sift flour, baking powder, and salt directly into the chocolate mixture. Gently stir until dry ingredients are barely incorporated.
6. Cut the 6 oz of semi-sweet chocolate into 1/2 inch chunks and stir into dough along with the toasted nuts.
7. Lightly butter 4 aluminum baking sheets (do not use foil or cookies will burn). Drop dough by tablespoonfuls, about 12 to a sheet, leaving room for cookies to spread.
8. Bake one sheet at a time in the center of oven at 350°F (175°C) for 10-12 minutes, or until cookies lose their shine. Cookies will still be very soft.
9. Allow cookies to cool for 2 minutes before removing from pans. Using an icing bag with a pin-sized tip, pipe melted semi-sweet chocolate onto cookies in a criss-cross pattern.
10. Allow to cool at least 1 hour before storing in tins.
Makes 50 one-ounce cookies
